ux_issues.description: Message 1 (arrival advice) did not mention SIM card or money exchange — two of the most critical first-steps for arriving in Bangkok. Only mentioned transport and keeping valuables safe.
ux_issues.suggestion: Companion mode arrival responses should cover the top 3-4 practical needs: transport, SIM/connectivity, cash/ATM, and accommodation check-in.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: AI attempted to call create_itinerary_items on message 5 ('recommend something fun for tonight') despite this being an informational query in companion mode. The companion gate correctly rejected it with status 'not_needed', and the AI re-generated without itinerary creation. The system safety net worked, but the AI should not have attempted the tool call in the first place.
ai_behavior_issues.context: Message 5 in companion mode asked for evening recommendations. AI called create_itinerary_items with a night market item before the gate blocked it.

usefulness_evaluation.narrative: Companion mode performed well overall. All responses were concise (77-114 words, well under 200-word target). The AI gave Thailand-specific advice including real restaurant names, Thai language phrases, specific baht amounts for tipping, and correct geographic reasoning (identified Wat Saket from 'big gold temple' description). The companion gate correctly blocked unsolicited itinerary creation and allowed explicit itinerary requests. The AI remembered the temple from earlier context when asked to add it to the itinerary. Minor gaps: arrival advice missed SIM card and money exchange, and the AI attempted (but was blocked from) creating itinerary items on an informational query.
